title: Beautiful World In a Church
wikitext:
[]: Beautiful World... In a Church!!!             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Sep 09, 2012 11:53 am So, just chit-chat about this weird thing that happened last day... I was walking my dog when I went pass a Church (and not quite a normal Church, more like one of those, let's call them "adoration centers" with no specific religion but still organized somehow)... Anyhow, they are normally singing stuff about Christ and salvation (and most of the time, all Sunday long), but the thing that caught mi attention this time is that I first heard a cord progression that I started humming without noticing... Then I heard the lyrics and fell into account of what they were playing... The group was playing "Beautiful World"... Yup... The closing theme of Rebuild movies... Well, a version of it with rather altered lyrics that changed lines like "you just dream with shounen manga" to "you just want your own happiness"... Overall, the song morphed into something about salvation and wanting to be with Christ in a "beautiful world" with no pain where we can be "all together now"... I stood there listening by a window, and after the song ended the group said thanks and goodbye, then someone else got on stage so I dunno if they said that the song was and, um, adaptation... Dunno what to make out of it... What do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Sep 09, 2012 2:00 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Sep 09, 2012 2:07 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: especially to go in and such

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Sep 09, 2012 3:33 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Sep 09, 2012 5:30 pm Dafuq? How'd they get the idea of using that song of all things? I don't see anything particularly religious about it, it's like, your basic love song with a little contemplation thrown in, but not of the religious sort. I don't like the idea of anyone "correcting" art for their own purposes, (unless it's a parody work which doesn't take itself seriously by definition where the "mockery" is openly part of the joke)regardless of what these purposes are, it's such a pretentious thing to do, like they think they can show the original authors how it's done. I really doubt they payed any royalties. No, dear random Church people, please come up with your melody as we secular people do! It's such a cheap thing to do. So who are they, some people praising a being they claim as the creator of this cosmos, or Weird al Yancovic? It becomes even more ridiculous when you realize that most people who were involved in producing this song most likely weren't Christian at all.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Sep 09, 2012 5:34 pm Kendrix wrote: Dafuq?I don't like the idea of anyone "correcting" art for their own purposes, it's such a pretentious thing to do

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Sep 09, 2012 5:52 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Sep 09, 2012 6:00 pm Imagine Asuka's mindrape without the Hallelujah chorus! Besides, no harm done... If I had not walked that afternoon of that day in a not very transited street of the city where I live in Mexico, probably no one would've made the connection between the song and Evangelion...

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sun Sep 09, 2012 10:44 pm I don't object to them using the song, but I do object to them rewriting it into an advertisement for their religion. That does cross a line, I think. Doesn't one of their pamphlets or somesuch talk about theft? Before we know it, Churches will be utilizing Cohen's "Hallelujah" in a completely inappropriate way...

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Sep 09, 2012 11:11 pm Warren Peace wrote: I don't object to them using the song, but I do object to them rewriting it into an advertisement for their religion.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Sep 10, 2012 12:15 am I don't think simply opening a Church entitles one to all the music ever associated with Christianity. NGE didn't take "Hallelujah" from the churches of the world, they took it from Handel. Gainax can't pay Handel, but they did pay for the specific recording.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Sep 10, 2012 12:20 am The lyrics of those song come from some Christian books or something. They also heavily took inspiration from Judaic/Christian symbolism and traditions. Anyway the difference is that those things, both musics and lyrics, are public domain, but do you think that a small church gives something about copyright?

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Sep 10, 2012 12:26 am I know, I brought up "Hallelujah" because it's most commonly referenced as far as Eva "stealing" from Christianity and what not [EDIT: he took out the part about Verdi so nevermind]. It's fine for them to use the song, but it's bad taste to rewrite that song to push a religion (or anything else). Sing "The Sound of Silence" all you want, just don't make it "The Sound of Jesus".

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Sep 10, 2012 12:43 am Warren Peace wrote: [EDIT: he took out the part about Verdi so nevermind].

[]: Double-Standard. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Sep 10, 2012 1:11 am Warren Peace wrote: I don't object to them using the song, but I do object to them rewriting it into an advertisement for their religion.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Mon Sep 10, 2012 1:15 am Kendrix wrote: I don't like the idea of anyone "correcting" art for their own purposes, (unless it's a parody work which doesn't take itself seriously by definition where the "mockery" is openly part of the joke)regardless of what these purposes are, it's such a pretentious thing to do, like they think they can show the original authors how it's done. I really doubt they payed any royalties.

[]: Re: Double-Standard. Bl             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Sep 10, 2012 3:23 am Alaska Slim wrote: Are you going to complain about this too ? There really isn't any difference.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Sep 10, 2012 3:34 am ^ Actually you made me laugh a lot, it would have been funny if they did it before actually killing him. Alaska Slim wrote: Fair use, this can certainly qualify. They aren't likely recording this and selling it on an album anyway.

[]: Re: Double-Standard. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Sep 10, 2012 3:50 am Warren Peace wrote: There is a big one: it doesn't change the song (AFAIK), merely presents it in a new context. If there was a new version called "Call Me Definitely (To Kill Bin Laden)", that would be comparable.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Sep 10, 2012 6:11 am I'm not talking the legality of what they're doing. I'm pretty sure they have the legal right (though with the craziness of copyright these days, who really knows). Outside that, I can't see how one can deny they're taking the original framework and stuffing their own message into it. You may not call that "stealing" as I do. Whatever it is, I just wish they had the basic respect for the artist not to do it. It's in bad taste at the very least.
